figures.caption: FIG. 2. Localized eigenmode spectra of an asymmetric antibimeron with topological charge Q = +1 as a function of external magnetic field. Panel (a) shows the in-plane field Bx dependence, and (b) shows the out-of-plane field Bz dependence. The shaded blue region corresponds to the magnon continuum.

FIG. 3. Gyration of the topological charge density peaks in asymmetric antibimerons (AABs). Topological charge density distribution for AAB with (a) Q=+1 (b) Q=-1. Red dots and loops represent the minima (a) and maxima (b) of the topological charge, along with their corresponding trajectories over one period of AAB oscillation. Reversing the topological charge (Q→ −Q) inverts the density, q(r) → −q(r), such that q(a) max = |q (b) min|. Black arrows indicate the direction of rotation associated with the gyrotropic mode.

FIG. 1. Asymmetric antibimerons (AABs) and their effective mechanical representation. a Magnetization configuration of a single AAB. b AAB cluster containing N = 3 antibimerons. In panels (a) and (b), colors indicate the out-of-plane magnetization component. c Schematic of the corresponding one-dimensional spring-mass model describing AAB dynamics. Each AAB is represented as a meron dimer (outlined in orange) comprising two merons (red) coupled by an intra-dimer spring (blue), while neighbouring dimers are connected by inter-dimer springs (black).

FIG. 5. Mechanical representation of asymmetric antibimeron (AAB) clusters and corresponding normal-mode spectra. a Spring-mass schematic of the meron-dimer model for an AAB cluster with N = 3. Each AAB is modeled as two particles (corresponding to merons) of mass m, labeled a and b, connected by an intradimer spring with stiffness kin. Adjacent dimers are coupled by an inter-dimer spring with stiffness kc. Grey dashed contours schematically indicate the underlying spin textures. b Normal-mode spectra of the coupled-oscillator model for clusters containing up to N = 5 dimers, shown for kin = kc. c Dependence of the eigenmode frequencies on the coupling ratio kc/kin for a three-dimer (N = 3) chain. In panels (b) and (c), the frequency ω is expressed in units of ω1 = √ 2kin/m.

FIG. 4. Asymmetric antibimeron (AAB) clusters and their power spectra. a Magnetization configurations of AAB clusters with N ranging from 2 to 5. The colors indicate the out-of-plane magnetization component. b Power spectra S ( f ) of AAB clusters. The bottom curve represents an isolated AAB for comparison, while the remaining colored curves correspond to the clusters containing 2 to 5 AABs.
